title: carya alba var. anomala
intro: Carya alba var. anomala, also known as the white hickory or white shagbark hickory, is a plant species within the Carya genus. It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, supporting cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Carya alba var. anomala include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various ailments, including arthritis, fever, and digestive issues.
